items.title: ‘I can still feel him’: Gurrumul’s posthumous honour brings pride for family

children.content: Nearly a decade since his death, Gurrumul is set to be inducted into the ARIA Hall of Fame, bringing pride and reflection to a remote part of Australia.

items.title: Advocates say 'three big gorillas' have stopped real national gambling reform

children.content: Under the cover of budget day, the Albanese government this week dropped its long-awaited response to a gambling harm inquiry. Advocates say it has "failed miserably".

items.title: Police make arrest over alleged stabbing of man sleeping at home in NT town

children.content: A 23-year-old man has been arrested over the alleged stabbing of another man who was sleeping in a home in remote Tennant Creek about 990 kilometres south-east of Darwin.

image.altText: A close-up shows flashing lights at night time on top of a police car.

items.title: Amid grief, the NT's CLP rushes drastic child protection changes

children.content: Stakeholders have just one week to respond to sweeping changes to NT child protection laws that Aboriginal groups say could do "generations" of harm.

articles.title: National commissioner says review into Kumanjayi Little Baby won't 'cut it'

children.content: The National Commissioner for Indigenous children has criticised the selection of a retired NSW police commissioner and long-standing NT public servant as the leaders of a review into the territory's child protection system.

items.title: Diphtheria is back in Australia. Here's what to know

children.content: Once a major cause of childhood deaths, diphtheria was largely eliminated through vaccination. Health authorities say falling immunity may now be helping the disease spread again.

articles.title: Australia records first reported death from diphtheria in almost a decade

children.content: The Northern Territory has recorded the country's first reported death from diphtheria in almost a decade, as experts urge people to get vaccinated.

image.altText: The hallway inside a hospital's emergency department.

items.title: Police to hold children up to 48 hours under NT youth justice changes

children.content: Northern Territory Police will be able to hold children in watch houses for up to 48 hours and question them without an adult present under planned changes to the Youth Justice Act.

image.altText: Hands grasping a cyclone wire fence.

items.title: Cattle industry loses court appeal over live export ban compensation

children.content: Australia's northern cattle industry says it is "deeply disappointed" by a Federal Court decision to dismiss its appeal over compensation for the 2011 live export ban.

image.altText: the sun sets over cattle cattle yard with dust rising

items.title: Northern Territory musicians escape to international festival in the UK

children.content: Northern Territory musicians attending The Great Escape music festival in the UK are hoping their experience will open more doors for regional artists.

items.title: Man with swastika tattooed on leg arrested in Alice Springs

children.content: A 25-year-old man has been arrested and charged in Alice Springs for displaying a tattoo of a swastika on his leg.

items.title: First home buyers 'up and about' at auctions after negative gearing changes

children.content: The government says the changes are aimed at levelling the playing field for young buyers bidding against property investors, while the opposition is pledging to undo the reforms, citing their potential to raise rents.

image.altText: A row of houses on a suburban street in Melbourne, in what looks like a colder month.

items.title: AI psychosis is on the rise and experts are sounding the alarm

children.content: Rodrigues thought an AI chatbot could help him escape poverty and build wealth. Instead, he became trapped in a spiral of delusion and dependence.

items.title: China quietly turns off supply of world's most used industrial chemical

children.content: Amid disruption from the Iran war, China has banned exports of sulphuric acid, a crucial component used to treat drinking water, refine petroleum and make batteries, clothing and phosphate fertilisers.

items.title: Top Hamas military leader killed in Israeli air strikes, official confirms

children.content: Izz al-Din al-Haddad is the most senior Hamas official to be killed by Israel since a US-backed ceasefire agreement meant to halt fighting was implemented in October.

items.title: Divers who faced jail for cultural fishing lead sea urchin recovery

children.content: After decades of fighting for their traditional fishing rights, Aboriginal custodians are now fighting to save their sea country from ecological collapse.

items.title: 'You're malnourished; you are overtrained': Olympic gymnast's warning

children.content: Alexandra Kiroi-Bogatyreva trained up to 10 hours a day, six days a week, often without the proper nutrition. She wants others to know what that can do to the body.

items.title: Sam Kerr bids emotional Chelsea farewell with record-equalling goal

items.title: Artist appointed, axed then reinstated from Venice Biennale triumphs

children.content: A Gold Coast council report finds an e-bike crackdown has failed to significantly reduce anti-social behaviour, with many riders ignoring compliance officers.

items.title: From cask to car, could red wine be Australia's next fuel?

items.title: You could hear a pin drop when Rob revealed why he fought a fire tax

children.content: Every time farmer and volunteer firefighter Rob Armstrong gets a call to battle a bushfire, something inside says not to do it. But he goes anyway. He knows what it’s like to be trapped in the flames and rescued by volunteers.

items.title: Major highways left in the dark as copper theft surges in Queensland
